name: Needle
description: Distilled from Gemini 2.5 Flash. An attention-only transformer that maps natural language to structured function calls. 15 tool categories. Designed for edge inference: 250 MB checkpoint, runs on CPU in under 50 ms per call. Zero API dependency at inference.
author: @cactus
source_paper_title: Needle — Distilling Gemini Tool Calling into a 26M Model

pytorch.description: For training continuation, HuggingFace, custom Python.

onnx.description: CPU/GPU inference, edge, browser via onnxruntime-web.

gguf.description: Quantized for llama.cpp, mobile, embedded targets.

strengths: Strong tool-name selection on imperative queries (Set a timer..., Send a message to...)
High precision on argument keys (0.85) — when it produces an arg, it's usually a real one
Holds JSON structure consistently — never produces malformed nesting
Gracefully handles 3-6 tools in context window (the typical user scenario)
Inference cost: ~$0/call, runs CPU-only, 45ms median latency
weaknesses: Confused between adjacent social tools (predicts Twitter for an Instagram prompt)
Arg-key recall (0.64) — sometimes drops optional but useful args like unit
Exact-call accuracy only 3% — value-level prediction is the soft spot
WordPiece tokenizer introduces whitespace noise in decoded JSON
Untested on chained 2-tool calls (~20% of training data, untested in eval)

samples.utterance: Hey, can you log my weight? I'm at 75 kilograms now.
Post on my Instagram story: 'Beach blast!'
Text my wife, 'Don't forget the milk!'
What's the weather in Lisbon tomorrow?

name: PII Guard
description: Context-aware PII detection. Spots names, addresses, SSNs, credit cards in user prompts before they hit your LLM provider. Trained on synthetic data from Claude. CPU-runnable, sub-50 ms scan.
author: @house

name: Claimant
description: Distilled fact-checking head. Given a claim and a small context, produces a calibrated belief score with citations. Useful as a guardrail layer in front of long-form generation.
author: @house

name: Routor
description: Multi-label intent router for agent frameworks. Given a user message, predicts which downstream tool/agent should handle it. 8M params, hilariously fast.
author: @nordic
